supply_chain.dependencies: The Company relies upon sole or limited sources of supply for certain materials, components and services. Some products require particular specifications or regulatory qualifications where there may be a single supplier or limited number of suppliers.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions due to supplier capacity constraints, bankruptcy, decreased availability of key raw materials, natural disasters, pandemic health issues, war, terrorist actions, governmental actions such as trade protectionism. Reliance on third-party package delivery services (FedEx, DHL) for cold-chain shipping. Manufacturing complexity for certain products subject to FDA regulations.

regulatory_risks: FDA regulation of medical devices and diagnostic products - failure to comply could result in warning letters, recalls, monetary sanctions, injunctions
EU In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation (EU IVDR) - stricter requirements for marketing in vitro diagnostic devices
CLIA certification requirements for laboratory developed tests
U.S. Federal Anti-Kickback Statute and healthcare fraud regulations
HIPAA privacy and security regulations - breaches could result in significant fines
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and state data privacy laws
GDPR in Europe - significant fines for non-compliance up to €20 million or 4% of annual turnover
Export/import control regulations enforced by U.S. Departments of Commerce, State and Treasury
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and UK Bribery Act compliance
Environmental health and safety laws governing hazardous materials
Changes in healthcare reimbursement policies affecting diagnostic tests
Potential FDA regulation of lab developed tests
Government negotiation/regulation of drug prices affecting pharmaceutical customers
Data localization laws in China, Russia and other countries requiring local data storage
Open Payments Act reporting requirements for medical device manufacturers

sanction_risks: Russia military invasion of Ukraine and response sanctions causing economic disruption
Israel conflict in Gaza creating humanitarian and economic crises
U.S.-China trade tensions with tariffs on goods imported between countries
Potential technology export restrictions affecting China market access
Economic sanctions restricting trade with certain countries
Restrictions on government procurement in certain jurisdictions
